visa_types.name: Tourist Visit
visa_types.documents_required:
Documents to be submitted by all applicants for Tourist Visit:
- Completed
Electronic Visa Application Form (https://visa.vrm.lt/epm/)
, one recent passport photo 35×45 colorful, white
                                                                            background.One recent passport photo 35×45 colorful, white background.
- A valid travel document - passport (containing at least 2 blank pages, issued within the previous 10 years, its period of validity extending at least three months after the intended
                                                                            date of departure from the territory if the Member States). Non-Sri Lankan nationals residing in Sri Lanka shall also present the temporary or permanent residence permit granted
                                                                            to the applicant.
- Valid health insurance, covering the duration of stay in Schengen area (in case of multi-entry visas – for the first trip). The minimum amount of medical insurance coverage – EUR
                                                                            30 000.
- Number of invitation letter for the alien to temporarily stay in the Republic of Lithuania electronically submitted through
EPIS (https://epis.vrm.lt/)
or
                                                                            the documents confirming the purpose and conditions of the planned visit to the Republic of Lithuania (hotel booking, tour itinerary etc.).
- Proof of means of transport. Flight reservation of return or round ticket. If the applicant is travelling to several Schengen States, proof of intra-Schengen flight reservation, train
                                                                            itinerary or car rental.
- Proof of lodging. Hotel reservations, rental of holiday home or campus residence reservation. If the applicant intends to stay with a family member or a friend, proof of sponsorship
                                                                            and/or private accommodation from the host. If applicant is travelling to several Schengen States, proof of lodging in each of them.
- Proof of financial means: original private bank statement showing movements in the last three months with balance confirmation letter, duly stamped and signed by the bank;
- Proof of real estate property or residence: Gramasevaka letter certified by the divisional secretary.
- To obtain a multiple-entry short-stay visa, a foreign national must submit documents substantiating of the necessity for such visa to or documents substantiating the circumstances
                                                                            entitling him to a multiple-entry short-stay visa.
- Consular fee.
In addition:
- If the applicant is employed:
- payslips for the last three months;
- employment contract;
- documentary proof of EPF contribution
- employers statement on approval for holidays.
- If the applicant is a company owner or self-employed:
- certificate of registration of the company
- company’s bank statements for the last three months

Overview
Lithuania Tourist visa issued for those going to Lithuania for holidays, tourism, entertainment or to see friends and family in Lithuania.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Lithuania Tourist visa issued for those going to Lithuania for holidays, tourism, entertainment or to see friends and family in Lithuania. 

## Visa Fees
Visa Fees applicable for Tourist Visit is:

[Table:
                                                                           Tourist Visit]
The above mentioned Visa Fee should be paid in Cash.
VFS Service charge:
Applicants applying at the Lithuanian Visa Application Centre shall be levied a service charge of 15570LKR
Please Note:
- The applicable visa fee in Sri Lankan Rupees is as per the current exchange rate. The same is subject to change without notice.
- The Visa fee and service charges are non-refundable in case the visa is refused.

## Documents Required
Documents to be submitted by all applicants for Tourist Visit:
- Completed
Electronic Visa Application Form (https://visa.vrm.lt/epm/)
, one recent passport photo 35×45 colorful, white
                                                                            background.One recent passport photo 35×45 colorful, white background.
- A valid travel document - passport (containing at least 2 blank pages, issued within the previous 10 years, its period of validity extending at least three months after the intended
                                                                            date of departure from the territory if the Member States). Non-Sri Lankan nationals residing in Sri Lanka shall also present the temporary or permanent residence permit granted
                                                                            to the applicant.
- Valid health insurance, covering the duration of stay in Schengen area (in case of multi-entry visas – for the first trip). The minimum amount of medical insurance coverage – EUR
                                                                            30 000.
- Number of invitation letter for the alien to temporarily stay in the Republic of Lithuania electronically submitted through
EPIS (https://epis.vrm.lt/)
or
                                                                            the documents confirming the purpose and conditions of the planned visit to the Republic of Lithuania (hotel booking, tour itinerary etc.).
- Proof of means of transport. Flight reservation of return or round ticket. If the applicant is travelling to several Schengen States, proof of intra-Schengen flight reservation, train
                                                                            itinerary or car rental.
- Proof of lodging. Hotel reservations, rental of holiday home or campus residence reservation. If the applicant intends to stay with a family member or a friend, proof of sponsorship
                                                                            and/or private accommodation from the host. If applicant is travelling to several Schengen States, proof of lodging in each of them.
- Proof of financial means: original private bank statement showing movements in the last three months with balance confirmation letter, duly stamped and signed by the bank;
- Proof of real estate property or residence: Gramasevaka letter certified by the divisional secretary.
- To obtain a multiple-entry short-stay visa, a foreign national must submit documents substantiating of the necessity for such visa to or documents substantiating the circumstances
                                                                            entitling him to a multiple-entry short-stay visa.
- Consular fee.
In addition:
- If the applicant is employed:
- payslips for the last three months;
- employment contract;
- documentary proof of EPF contribution
- employers statement on approval for holidays.
- If the applicant is a company owner or self-employed:
- certificate of registration of the company
- company’s bank statements for the last three months

## Photo Specifications
 Photographs 
- The photograph must be in colour.
- It should have a white background.
- It should have a close up of your head and top of your shoulders so that your face takes up 70-80% of the photograph.]
- It should be in sharp focus and clean.
- It should be of high quality with no ink marks and creases.
- The photograph must show you looking directly at the camera.
- It should be 3.5 cm X 4.5 cm.
- It should show your skin tone naturally.
- It should have appropriate brightness and contrast.
- The photograph should be a recent one not more than 6 months old.
- It is to be taken without headgear.
- Photograph can be taken with headgear, after consideration on case to case basis if the applicant wears headgear on religious grounds. The main characteristics of the face must be
                                                                            apparent from the photograph. Chin, nose, eyes and eyebrows must not be covered; the forehead should be uncovered to the extent that the shape of the face is apparent.
- It should be without hat/cap.
- It should be without the applicant's hair tucked behind his/her ears.
- The photograph should not have any staple marks.
- Scanned and colour photocopies would NOT be accepted.

## Processing Time
Please apply at least 3 weeks in advance and note that visa processing takes up to 14 working days if additional consultation is not required.

## Online Application Form
Electronic Visa Application  Form (https://visa.vrm.lt/epm/)

- Completed and signed Schengen visa application form. (

                                                                            equals to 1 minimum monthly wage)
- A confirmed Air ticket reservation.
- Valid health insurance, covering the duration of stay. It must be valid in all participant states of the Schengen Area. The minimum amount of medical insurance coverage – EUR 30 000.
- Consular fee.

visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Temporary residence permit is usually issued for 1 or 2 years. However, it might be issued for a shorter period. For the persons of Lithuanian origin and foreigners who have retained
                                                                        the right for the Lithuanian citizenship, temporary residence permit is granted for 5 years. 

## Visa Fees
Visa Fees applicable for Residence Permit is:

[Table:
                                                                           Residence Permit]
The above mentioned Visa Fee should be paid in Cash.
VFS Service charge:
Applicants applying at the Lithuanian Visa Application Centre shall be levied a service charge of 15570LKR
Please Note:
- The applicable visa fee in Sri Lankan Rupees is as per the current exchange rate. The same is subject to change without notice.
- The Visa fee and service charges are non-refundable in case the visa is refused.

## Documents Required
Documents to be submitted by all applicants for Residence Permit:
- Completed and signed
National visa application form (https://visa.vrm.lt/epm/)
.
- One recent passport photo 35×45 colorful, white background.
- A valid travel document - passport (containing at least 2 blank pages, issued within the previous 10 years, its period of validity extending at least three months after the intended
                                                                            date of departure from the territory if the Member States).
- Valid health insurance, covering the duration of stay. It must be valid in all participant states of the Schengen Area. The minimum amount of medical insurance coverage – EUR 30 000.
- A Report of a decision adopted by Migration Department under the Ministry of the Interior of the Republic of Lithuania to issue a Residence Permit in Lithuania or a Report of a lost
                                                                            Residence permit card.

## Photo Specifications
 Photographs 
- The photograph must be in colour.
- It should have a white background.
- It should have a close up of your head and top of your shoulders so that your face takes up 70-80% of the photograph.]
- It should be in sharp focus and clean.
- It should be of high quality with no ink marks and creases.
- The photograph must show you looking directly at the camera.
- It should be 3.5 cm X 4.5 cm.
- It should show your skin tone naturally.
- It should have appropriate brightness and contrast.
- The photograph should be a recent one not more than 6 months old.
- It is to be taken without headgear.
- Photograph can be taken with headgear, after consideration on case to case basis if the applicant wears headgear on religious grounds. The main characteristics of the face must be
                                                                            apparent from the photograph. Chin, nose, eyes and eyebrows must not be covered; the forehead should be uncovered to the extent that the shape of the face is apparent.
- It should be without hat/cap.
- It should be without the applicant's hair tucked behind his/her ears.
- The photograph should not have any staple marks.
- Scanned and colour photocopies would NOT be accepted.

## Processing Time
Please apply at least 3 weeks in advance and note that visa processing takes up to 14 working days if additional consultation is not required.

- The applicable visa fee in Sri Lankan Rupees is as per the current exchange rate. The same is subject to change without notice.
- The Visa fee and service charges are non-refundable in case the visa is refused.

## Documents Required
Documents to be submitted by all applicants for Airport Transit:
- Completed
Electronic Visa Application Form (https://visa.vrm.lt/epm/)
, one recent passport photo 35×45 colorful, white
                                                                            background.One recent passport photo 35×45 colorful, white background.
- A valid travel document - passport (containing at least 2 blank pages, issued within the previous 10 years, its period of validity extending at least three months after the intended
                                                                            date of departure from the territory if the Member States). Non-Sri Lankan nationals residing in Sri Lanka shall also present the temporary or permanent residence permit granted
                                                                            to the applicant.
- Valid health insurance, covering the duration of stay in Schengen area (in case of multi-entry visas – for the first trip). The minimum amount of medical insurance coverage – EUR
                                                                            30 000.
- Number of invitation letter for the alien to temporarily stay in the Republic of Lithuania electronically submitted through
EPIS (https://epis.vrm.lt/)
or
                                                                            the documents confirming the purpose and conditions of the planned visit to the Republic of Lithuania (hotel booking, tour itinerary etc.).
- Proof of means of transport. Flight reservation of return or round ticket. If the applicant is travelling to several Schengen States, proof of intra-Schengen flight reservation, train
                                                                            itinerary or car rental.
- Proof of lodging. Hotel reservations, rental of holiday home or campus residence reservation. If the applicant intends to stay with a family member or a friend, proof of sponsorship
                                                                            and/or private accommodation from the host. If applicant is travelling to several Schengen States, proof of lodging in each of them.
- Proof of financial means: original private bank statement showing movements in the last three months with balance confirmation letter, duly stamped and signed by the bank;
- Proof of real estate property or residence: Gramasevaka letter certified by the divisional secretary.
- To obtain a multiple-entry short-stay visa, a foreign national must submit documents substantiating of the necessity for such visa to or documents substantiating the circumstances
                                                                            entitling him to a multiple-entry short-stay visa.
- Consular fee.
In addition:
- If the applicant is employed:
- payslips for the last three months;
- employment contract;
- documentary proof of EPF contribution
- employers statement on approval for holidays.
- If the applicant is a company owner or self-employed:
- certificate of registration of the company
- company’s bank statements for the last three months
- If the applicant is sponsored:
- proof of sponsorship and relevant financial documents of sponsoring individual/company.
- If the applicant is retired:
- pension statements for the last three months;
- proof of regular income generated by ownership of property or business.
- If the applicant is a minor:
- if a minor travels alone or only with one parent: consent of parental authority or legal guardian and birth certificate and copies of parents’ passports or District
                                                                                    Court appointment decree

## Photo Specifications
 Photographs 
- The photograph must be in colour.
- It should have a white background.
- It should have a close up of your head and top of your shoulders so that your face takes up 70-80% of the photograph.]
- It should be in sharp focus and clean.
- It should be of high quality with no ink marks and creases.
- The photograph must show you looking directly at the camera.
- It should be 3.5 cm X 4.5 cm.
- It should show your skin tone naturally.
- It should have appropriate brightness and contrast.
- The photograph should be a recent one not more than 6 months old.
- It is to be taken without headgear.
- Photograph can be taken with headgear, after consideration on case to case basis if the applicant wears headgear on religious grounds. The main characteristics of the face must be
                                                                            apparent from the photograph. Chin, nose, eyes and eyebrows must not be covered; the forehead should be uncovered to the extent that the shape of the face is apparent.
- It should be without hat/cap.
- It should be without the applicant's hair tucked behind his/her ears.
- The photograph should not have any staple marks.
- Scanned and colour photocopies would NOT be accepted.

## Processing Time
Please apply at least 3 weeks in advance and note that visa processing takes up to 14 working days if additional consultation is not required.

visa_types.name: Visa Types
visa_types.overview:
If you are planning a short visit to Lithuania and you do not have Lithuanian nationality, you may need to apply for a Schengen visa. Lithuania is one of the 26 European countries forming the Schengen
                                                            Area which have no border controls between them. These countries also issue a common visa, the Schengen visa.
visa_types.full_text:
If you are planning a short visit to Lithuania and you do not have Lithuanian nationality, you may need to apply for a Schengen visa. Lithuania is one of the 26 European countries forming the Schengen
                                                            Area which have no border controls between them. These countries also issue a common visa, the Schengen visa.
